.github/workflows/publish.yml.general_idea: Change JDK version from 13 to 17 to align with the migration target.
.github/workflows/publish.yml.why_exists: Compilation failure on JDK 21 target if source is compiled with incompatible older JDK settings or if the build requires JDK 17+ features.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java source and target version to 21 to align with the JDK 21 migration.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project compiles against Java 17, failing to utilize or enforce Java 21 language features and bytecode compatibility required by the target environment.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Kotlin compiler plugin's jvmTarget to 17 to match the project's baseline Java version.
pom.xml.why_exists: Compiling Kotlin code with jvmTarget 1.8 against Java 17+ libraries can cause runtime compatibility issues or warnings; aligning it ensures consistent bytecode generation.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Kotlin version to 2.0.0 to support the new JDK 21 environment and modern Kotlin features.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older Kotlin versions may not fully support or compile correctly against JDK 21, or may lack necessary bug fixes for the new runtime.
